'''Dean Hadley''' (born 5 August 1992) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr|lf|pr}} and {{rlp|hk}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He has played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]], and [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Clarke|first=Paul|date=2018-06-29|title=Milestone man Hadley's journey from Boulevard terraces to FC regular|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/milestone-man-dean-hadleys-journey-1727712|access-date=2022-02-21|website=HullLive|language=en}}</ref>

==Background==
Hadley was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], East Yorkshire, England.

==Career==
Hadley began his career with [[Hull F.C.]], coming through their academy and signing his first professional contract before the start of the 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/233054/dean-hadley|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130523004430/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/233054/dean-hadley|url-status=dead|archive-date=23 May 2013|title=Dean Hadley|access-date=1 July 2014|publisher=[[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]}}</ref> He plays as a {{rlp|sr}}.

He was loaned to [[York City Knights]], and made 10 appearances for the club, scoring 4 tries. He also played twice in the [[Super League]] in April for [[Hull F.C.]] due to injuries occurring in the first team.<ref name="Love">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Dean-Hadley|title=Dean Hadley|access-date=1 July 2014|website=Loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

For the 2014 season, he was loaned to [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]. He played just 3 games before being recalled to play in the first team for [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. Since then, he has made 8 appearances for the club and could be finally cementing his place in the starting 13. Dean has scored 2 tries for the club, one of them against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in a 30-6 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/26939155|title=Super League: Hull FC 30-6 Huddersfield Giants|access-date=1 July 2014|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=10 April 2014}}</ref>

On 29 July 2019, Hadley joined rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] from [[Hull F.C.]] on a three-year deal, teammate [[Jez Litten]] also joined the red and whites on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-duo-join-rivals-hull-kr-with-immediate-effect|title=Hull FC duo join rivals Hull KR with immediate effect|date=29 July 2019|access-date=13 August 2024|website=Loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Hadley played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Hadley played a total of 23 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Hadley played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Hadley played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 September 2025, Hadley played in Hull Kingston Rovers final game of the regular season seeing the club win the League Leaders Shield in victory over Warrington <ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-18 |title=Super League: Hull KR 28-20 Warrington Wolves: Robins win League Leaders' Shield |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo |access-date=2026-03-05 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Hadley played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 19 February 2026, Hadley played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|website=Loverugbyleague.com|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea at the Lae Football Stadium]].<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|website=Wwos.nine.vom.au|date=27 October 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>
